title: Consider defining media type for container signature format
body:
I am curious if anyone has given any thought to defining a media type for the [container signature format](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/master/docs/containers-signature.5.md). I'd like to be able to reference these from a [content descriptor](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/master/descriptor.md), but I need a corresponding `mediaType`.

If you're not interested in a _ton_ of overhead, you should be able to use the [vendor tree](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6838#section-3.2) to define something like:

```
application/vnd.<some-redhat-specific-thing>+json
```

And then the rest of the internet can understand that to mean precisely the struct that you've defined in the docs, serialized as JSON. Most of the OCI media types are just defined to be what they are in a GitHub repo, but there is an actual registration process that Steve went through for [the image manifest](https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json), see:

>    While public exposure and review of media types to be registered in
>    the vendor tree are not required, using the media-types@iana.org
>    mailing list for review is encouraged, to improve the quality of
>    those specifications.  Registrations in the vendor tree may be
>    submitted directly to the IANA, where they will undergo Expert Review
>    [RFC5226] prior to approval.

If you _are_ interesting in a ton of overhead, I believe you could also go through the RFC process (or similar) to get this into the [standards tree](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6838#section-3.1).

For my purposes, I'd be satisfied with just some string that y'all make up that conforms to the RFC, but you may want to formally register it as well.

[This comment](https://github.com/containers/image/pull/59/files#r86455451) hits on why this would be useful, a bit. I couldn't find any other relevant discussion using GitHub's search tool, but let me know if I've missed anything.

cc @mtrmac

comments.body: @jonjohnsonjr I’m sorry, it seems no-one was around at the time to discuss this, and we need something like that now. Did you choose a value for your purposes?
